title: 3.1.1 Inorganic Molecules: Water
description: Properties and importance of water as a biochemical molecule.

questions.question: What is the chemical formula of water?
How many hydrogen atoms are linked to a single oxygen atom in each water molecule?
What percentage of a cell's mass does water typically account for?
Approximately what percentage of the human body is water?
In a water molecule, which atom is more electronegative?
What type of charge does the oxygen atom bear in a water molecule?
What type of charge does each hydrogen atom bear in a water molecule?
Water is described as which type of molecule due to its unequal electron sharing?
What is the name of the interaction between the partially positive hydrogen of one water molecule and the partially negative oxygen of another water molecule?
What type of substances does water act as an effective solvent for?
How does water behave towards non-polar molecules such as lipids?
options: They form hydrogen bonds with water
They are insoluble in water and pushed together
questions.correctAnswer: They are insoluble in water and pushed together

questions.question: What is the specific heat capacity of water?
options: Amount of heat to melt one gram of ice
Amount of heat to vaporize one gram of water
Amount of heat to freeze one gram of water
Amount of heat to raise temperature of one gram of water by one degree Celsius
questions.correctAnswer: Amount of heat to raise temperature of one gram of water by one degree Celsius

questions.question: Why does water have a high specific heat capacity?
options: Water has a low molecular weight
Hydrogen bonds cause molecules to stick together requiring more energy to move
questions.correctAnswer: Hydrogen bonds cause molecules to stick together requiring more energy to move

questions.question: What biological advantage does water's high heat capacity provide?
options: It makes water boil at lower temperatures
It makes water less effective as a solvent
It makes water more resistant to temperature changes
questions.correctAnswer: It makes water more resistant to temperature changes

questions.question: What is latent heat of vaporization?
options: Energy required to freeze a liquid
Energy required to melt a solid
Energy required to dissolve a substance
Thermal energy required to vaporize a liquid from liquid to gas
questions.correctAnswer: Thermal energy required to vaporize a liquid from liquid to gas

questions.question: Why does water have a comparatively high latent heat of vaporization?
options: Water has a low specific heat capacity
Water molecules have no intermolecular forces
Hydrogen bonds must be broken before molecules can escape as gas
questions.correctAnswer: Hydrogen bonds must be broken before molecules can escape as gas

questions.question: How does the density of ice compare to liquid water?
options: Ice is less dense than liquid water
Ice and liquid water have the same density
Ice is denser than liquid water
questions.correctAnswer: Ice is less dense than liquid water

questions.question: At what temperature does water density begin to fall?
What is the ecological benefit of ice floating on liquid water?
options: It allows fish to breathe better
It insulates the water beneath and minimizes freezing of large water amounts
It increases the rate of evaporation
questions.correctAnswer: It insulates the water beneath and minimizes freezing of large water amounts

questions.question: What do changes in water density due to temperature generate in oceans?
options: Currents that aid circulation of nutrients
questions.correctAnswer: Currents that aid circulation of nutrients

questions.question: What term describes the tendency of water molecules to stick to each other?
What term describes the attraction of water molecules to other molecules?
What is capillary action?
options: Water climbing up a narrow tube due to adhesion
questions.correctAnswer: Water climbing up a narrow tube due to adhesion

questions.question: Why are cohesive and adhesive forces crucial for plants?
options: They are crucial for transport of water from roots to leaves
questions.correctAnswer: They are crucial for transport of water from roots to leaves

questions.question: What property of water results from high cohesion at the water surface?
Which organism can walk on the surface of water due to surface tension?
What is the boiling point of water?
What is the freezing point of water?
Why does water have a very high boiling point despite its low molecular weight?
options: Water requires more energy to break its hydrogen bonds before boiling
Water has a simple molecular structure
questions.correctAnswer: Water requires more energy to break its hydrogen bonds before boiling

questions.question: How does the ability of water to form hydrogen bonds affect its boiling point?
options: It has no effect on boiling point
It causes water to decompose before boiling

questions.question: Why are the boiling and freezing points of water important for aquatic ecosystems?
options: They increase the salt concentration in water
They prevent water from being easily frozen or boiled, protecting aquatic life
They cause water to become acidic
questions.correctAnswer: They prevent water from being easily frozen or boiled, protecting aquatic life

questions.question: Why can sweat keep our bodies cool?
options: Sweat has a low specific heat capacity
Evaporation of sweat uses latent heat of vaporization
questions.correctAnswer: Evaporation of sweat uses latent heat of vaporization

questions.question: What is the boiling point of ethanol compared to water?
What is the freezing point of hydrogen sulfide?
How do water molecules distribute themselves around a positively charged ion in solution?
options: Both ends face the ion equally
The molecule turns away from the ion

questions.question: How do water molecules distribute themselves around a negatively charged ion in solution?
options: The molecule dissolves the ion completely
Both hydrogen atoms point toward the ion
The hydrogen (δ+) faces away from the ion

questions.question: What role does hydrogen bonding play in water's solvent properties?
options: Water molecules are attracted to ions and polar molecules, causing them to separate
It prevents water from dissolving substances
questions.correctAnswer: Water molecules are attracted to ions and polar molecules, causing them to separate

questions.question: What happens to non-polar molecules when surrounded by water?
options: They form hydrogen bonds with water
They are pushed together because water molecules attract each other
questions.correctAnswer: They are pushed together because water molecules attract each other

questions.question: What are the two elements that compose a water molecule?
How does the polarity of water affect its properties?
What property allows water to store more energy for a given temperature increase?
